summary: Russia launched a large-scale missile and drone attack hitting multiple regions, with Ukrainian air defenses downing 284 targets. Finland announced a €128 million military aid package for Ukraine, adding to its prior €3.4 billion equipment support. A Russian port in Taganrog suffered a tanker fire after a night drone strike, and a fuel depot in Armavir was also attacked.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine with Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 Shahed‑type drones, Ukrainian defenses downed 284 targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Finland will provide a 33rd military aid package worth about €128 million, bringing its total defense equipment sent to Ukraine to €3.4 billion (Source: @UkraineNow/81542).
A tanker, fuel reservoir and administration building burned in Taganrog port after a night drone attack; a fuel depot in Armavir, Russia was also hit (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying enemy positions near Novoselivka and conducting clearance operations near several villages, claiming the enemy lost at least 46 km² (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).

Multiple Shahed and other attack drones were observed over northern Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, with several heading toward Kyiv and other key areas (Source: @kpszsu/63294, @kpszsu/63269, @kpszsu/63271).
Ukrainian air alerts were raised in Kyiv on 29 May, but no cruise missiles were reported over the capital at that time (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67275).
Russia claimed to have shot down 127 Ukrainian drones over its territory during the night (Source: @tass_agency/378193).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a coordinated missile and drone barrage, including Iskander‑M, H‑101 and 290 Shahed drones, with Ukrainian defenses reporting 284 interceptions. Finland announced a new €128 million military aid package, augmenting its €3.4 billion support to Ukraine. A night drone strike ignited a tanker and fuel facilities in Taganrog, while a Russian fuel depot in Armavir also suffered damage.

points: Ukrainian forces claimed to have liberated territory near Novoselivka and conducted clearance operations near Vorny, Sicheve, Piddubne, Tovste, Novokhatke, and Zelenyi Hai, estimating a loss of at least 46 km² for the enemy (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).

The Ukrainian Ministry of Defense reported that during the night attack, 5 H‑101 cruise missiles and 279 Shahed‑type drones were shot down, with 9 attack drones hitting seven locations across the country (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Air raid alerts were issued in Kyiv on 29 May, though no cruise missiles were observed over the capital at that time (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67275).

Ukrainian air defenses engaged multiple drone swarms over northern Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, with several Shahed and Gerbera drones intercepted (Source: @kpszsu/63294, @kpszsu/63269, @kpszsu/63271).
Ukrainian officials highlighted ongoing attacks on oil and gas infrastructure in Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk regions, describing them as almost daily (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30536).
Russia reported that 127 Ukrainian drones were shot down over its territory during the night (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Six Ukrainian drones were destroyed over Tula region, according to the regional governor (Source: @tass_agency/378184).
A Russian port in Taganrog experienced a tanker fire and damage to a fuel reservoir after a night drone attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
A fuel depot of the Southern Oil Company in Armavir, Krasnodar Krai, was also attacked by drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Russian authorities warned of potential drone incidents in Romania, stating the origin of the drones requires forensic analysis (Source: @tass_agency/378194).
Shahed and other attack drones were observed over Kharkiv and Sumy regions, targeting oil and gas infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30536).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying enemy positions near Novoselivka, close to the eastern front line (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).
Multiple cruise missiles and drones launched from Russian regions (Bryansk, Vologda, Kursk) struck across Ukraine, including eastern areas (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 5 H‑101 cruise missiles and numerous drones over the eastern sector (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Reactive UAVs were reported along the Belarusian border in northern Chernihiv, moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/63294).
Ukrainian radar detected Shahed drones over northern Chernihiv and Kyiv regions, with some heading toward Kyiv suburbs (Source: @kpszsu/63288).
Russian missile launch sites in Bryansk region fired Iskander‑M ballistic missiles toward Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian forces downed numerous drones over northern Ukraine, including in Chernihiv and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/63294, @kpszsu/63273).
A tanker and fuel infrastructure were hit in Taganrog port after a night drone strike (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
A fuel depot in Armavir, Krasnodar Krai, was attacked by drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Ukrainian air defenses reported intercepting cruise missiles and drones over southern regions, though specific locations were not detailed (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Ukrainian forces continue to face drone threats in the southern direction, with reports of Shahed attacks near the Black Sea coast (Source: @kpszsu/63271).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 284 aerial targets, including 5 H‑101 cruise missiles and 279 Shahed‑type drones, during a massive night attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Russia claimed to have downed 127 Ukrainian drones over its territory in the same period (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Six Ukrainian drones were destroyed over Russia's Tula region (Source: @tass_agency/378184).
Ukrainian radar reported multiple cruise missile launches from Russian Bryansk and Vologda regions targeting various Ukrainian regions (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian forces reported no cruise missile flights over Kyiv at the time of the alert, only ballistic missile overflights (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67275).

key_developments: Russia launched a coordinated attack using Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 Shahed‑type drones, with Ukrainian defenses reporting 284 interceptions (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Finland announced a new €128 million military aid package for Ukraine, bringing its total defense equipment support to €3.4 billion (Source: @UkraineNow/81542).
A night drone strike caused a tanker fire and damage to fuel infrastructure at Taganrog port, and a fuel depot in Armavir was also hit (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).

Ukrainian forces claimed to have liberated territory near Novoselivka and cleared enemy positions near several villages, estimating a loss of at least 46 km² for the adversary (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).
Russia reported shooting down 127 Ukrainian drones over its territory during the night (Source: @tass_agency/378193).

Multiple Shahed and other attack drones were observed over northern Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, with several heading toward Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/63294, @kpszsu/63269, @kpszsu/63271).
Ukrainian air alerts were raised in Kyiv on 29 May, but no cruise missiles were reported over the capital at that time (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67275).
sections.threat_assessment: The large-scale missile and drone barrage demonstrates Russia's capability to coordinate multi-vector attacks across Ukraine, stressing the need for robust air defense integration. Ukrainian forces are maintaining offensive momentum on the eastern front, reclaiming territory and inflicting measurable losses on Russian positions. Persistent drone activity along the northern border and over key logistical hubs indicates a continued threat to command and control infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: Finland's substantial aid package underscores growing Western material support, potentially enhancing Ukraine's defensive depth. Russian claims of high drone losses may be aimed at domestic propaganda, while actual damage to Russian port facilities highlights vulnerability to Ukrainian UAV strikes. Neighboring states remain vigilant, with reports of drone incidents in Romania prompting diplomatic scrutiny.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of missile launch sites in Bryansk and Vologda regions is critical for early warning of future attacks. The concentration of Shahed drones over northern and eastern sectors suggests a shift toward saturation tactics to overwhelm air defenses. Continued assessment of Ukrainian counteroffensive gains near Novoselivka will inform operational planning for both sides.
